ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT RHEEM MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR PERSONAL INJURY OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DECLARATION CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS (INSULATION) ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. EVEN IF MR. FUHRMAN'S DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE, WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IT MERELY CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE HEAT EXCHANGERS PLAINTIFF WORKED WITH AND AROUND WERE MANUFACTURED BY RHEEM. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS DISMISSED HIS CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: CATHY MEYER OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (TEL: 510-451-1580)

Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Thomas Pearson

RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION F/K/A THE DURIRON COMPANY AND DURCO INTERNATIONAL, INC., SUED HEREIN AS DOE , IMPROPERLY DENOMINATED HEREIN AS FLOWSERVE(US), INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Flowserve Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims against it. Mr. Spence's declaration, even if admissible, is not sufficient to allow defendant to sustain its burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Charles F. Harlow of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & CONWAY LLP (415-453-0555), appearing for defendant / moving party Rheem Manufacturing Company
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Thomas Pearson

RULING:
DEFENDANT RHEEM MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR PERSONAL INJURY OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Fuhrman's declaration is admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue whether the heat exchangers plaintiff worked with and around were manufactured by Rheem. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
